Algeria.text: Algeria squeezed through the group stage in dramatic, exhausting fashion — Mahrez equalised in the third minute of stoppage time to salvage a 3-3 draw with Austria, a match that swung wildly in both directions after Kalajdzic promptly restored the Austrian lead at 90+6' in a finish that ultimately counted for nothing. The Fennec Foxes advance to face Switzerland in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a tie that looks closer to a coin-flip than a formality, with Algeria's attacking instincts and vulnerability at the back setting up a nervy contest against a disciplined Swiss side. Survive that, and Colombia or Ghana await in the last sixteen, before a potential quarterfinal collision with Argentina — which would represent a genuine examination of whether this Algeria team is merely a survivor or something more serious.

Argentina.text: Argentina closed out Group J in familiar fashion, leading 2-1 at the half against Jordan before Messi settled any lingering nerves with a third on 80 minutes — a controlled, professional performance from the world champions. Cape Verde await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, a tie Argentina enter as heavy favourites; the African side are capable of disruption but lack the quality to trouble a side of this pedigree across 90 minutes. Should they advance as expected, Colombia or Switzerland could pose sterner questions at the quarterfinal stage, with Brazil or England lurking as potential semifinal opponents.

Australia.text: A goalless draw with Paraguay to close the group stage was hardly a rousing send-off, but Australia did enough across Group D to advance as runners-up and earn their place in the last 32. They face Egypt on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT in a tie that, on reputation and recent form, Australia will be expected to win, though the Egyptians are no pushover and a passive Socceroos performance could invite trouble. Survive that, and Argentina — potential Round of 16 opponents — would represent a formidable examination of just how far this side has genuinely progressed.

Austria.text: Austria scraped through Group J in the most dramatic fashion: a 3-3 draw with Algeria that saw Rainer Mahrez level in the 90th minute before Sasa Kalajdzic's injury-time equaliser snatched the point Austria needed — a passage earned by the skin of their teeth rather than any air of authority. Spain await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT, and few would bet against the Spaniards: Austria arrive as clear underdogs, their leaky defence having conceded six times in the group and likely to face searching examination from one of Europe's most technically accomplished sides. Should they somehow advance, Croatia or Portugal loom in the Round of 16 — further evidence that Austria's bracket offers no soft landings whatsoever.

Belgium.text: Belgium closed the group stage in emphatic fashion, dismantling New Zealand 5-1 in a performance that never felt in doubt — Trossard helping himself to a brace before the hour, with Lukaku and Saelemaekers adding gloss late on to underline Belgium's attacking depth. They meet Senegal in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 13:00 PT, a tie Belgium enter as clear favourites, though Senegal's pace and physicality make them dangerous enough to demand respect. Should the Red Devils advance, Portugal or Spain could await in the quarterfinal, the kind of heavyweight collision that would tell us everything about their genuine title ambitions.

Bosnia & Herzegovina.text: Bosnia & Herzegovina closed their group stage with authority, putting Qatar away 3-1 through a swift first-half double from Alajbegovic and Abunada before Mahmic sealed it late — a confident finish that was enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed sides. Now comes a considerably sterner test: the hosts, USA, in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 17:00 PT, a tie in which Bosnia enter as underdogs against a team buoyed by home support and genuine tournament ambition. Survive that, and Belgium or Senegal await in the last sixteen — with a potential quarterfinal against Portugal or Spain looming as the price of a deep run.

Brazil.text: Brazil edged past Japan in a Round of 32 tie that was far less comfortable than their group-stage form suggested — Sano's 29th-minute opener forced a Casemiro equaliser after the break, before Martinelli settled nerves with a stoppage-time winner that flattered the five-time champions slightly. Norway await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT, and while Brazil enter as clear favourites, the Norwegians are no soft touch — expect a physical, attritional examination rather than a procession. Should Brazil come through, England or Argentina loom as potential landmines deeper in the draw.

Canada.text: Canada survived a nervy Round of 32 against South Africa, a 1-0 win settled only by Eustáquio's stoppage-time goal that suggested more endurance than dominance. They face Morocco in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT — a genuine test against a side that has proven itself capable of unsettling far weightier nations, and this looks very much a coin-flip rather than safe passage. Win that and France could await in the quarterfinal, with Portugal or Spain lurking as potential semifinal opponents who would demand everything Canada have.

Cape Verde.text: Cape Verde ground out a third consecutive draw to close the group stage, a goalless stalemate against Saudi Arabia that was as cautious and compact as the record suggests — three points from three draws, never beaten, never particularly free-scoring. Now comes the hardest possible reward for finishing second: Argentina in the Round of 32, Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, a fixture that reads less like an opportunity than a reckoning. Should the Blue Sharks somehow navigate that, Australia or Egypt await in the Round of 16 on Tue Jul 7 09:00 PT — and a quarterfinal path could yet produce Colombia or Algeria.

Colombia.text: Colombia closed out Group K with a disciplined 0-0 draw against Portugal — a hard-fought, tactically cautious affair that nonetheless confirmed their place as group winners, the clean sheet against such opposition no small achievement. Ghana await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a matchup that favors Colombia on paper: the Ghanaians have the capacity to disrupt, but a side that just neutralized Portugal should carry this with composure. Survive that, and a quarterfinal collision with Argentina looms as the draw's most tantalizing possibility — a South American derby that would test Colombia's genuine title credentials.

Croatia.text: Croatia edged past Ghana in a nervy group finale, Luka Sucic giving them a first-half lead that Ghana cancelled out with seventeen minutes to go before Nikola Vlasic settled matters late — a win that flattered neither side but delivered second place in Group L. Now comes a considerably sterner examination: Portugal in the Round of 32, Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT, a tie in which the Iberian side will start as clear favourites, their pedigree and firepower making this a genuine test of whether Croatia's tournament survival instincts can outlast a heavyweight. Win that, and Spain or Austria await in the last sixteen — with France or Morocco lurking as possible semifinal opponents should Zlatko Dalić's side find another gear.

Curaçao.text: Curaçao's group stage ended with a 0-2 defeat to Ivory Coast, Nicolas Pépé doing the damage with goals either side of half-time — the first arriving inside seven minutes to set a tone the Curaçao defence never recovered from. They finished fourth in Group E with a single point and a goal difference of minus nine, yet still advanced to the Round of 32 as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides. There, their journey came to an abrupt end, the gulf in quality across the group stage ultimately a true reflection of where this Curaçao side stands on the world stage.

Czech Republic.text: Czech Republic's group stage ended with a thumping 3-0 defeat to Mexico, the goals arriving in a clinical second-half burst and a stoppage-time finish that underlined the gulf in class — a deflating exit for a side that managed just one point across three matches. Having finished fourth in Group A and crept through as one of the best third-placed teams, they were nonetheless unable to sustain that precarious foothold, and Mexico's composed, purposeful display exposed their limitations emphatically. The Czechs depart at the earliest opportunity, their tournament leaving little to build on.

DR Congo.text: DR Congo closed the group stage with a statement: trailing to Shomurodov's early strike, they rallied to beat Uzbekistan 3-1, Wissa converting a penalty and then sealing it in stoppage time to confirm their passage as one of the best third-placed teams. England await in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 09:00 PT — a formidable assignment against one of the tournament's genuine contenders, with Gareth Southgate's side heavily favored to advance. Should DR Congo produce the upset, Ecuador or Mexico could follow in the Round of 16, with Brazil or Norway a possible quarterfinal obstacle beyond that.

Ecuador.text: Ecuador closed the group stage with a result that will reverberate — trailing to Leroy Sané's second-minute opener, they recovered to beat Germany 2-1, Angulo equalising early and Plata sealing it with thirteen minutes to spare in what became a composed, controlled second-half performance. That victory lifts them into the Round of 32 as one of the better third-placed qualifiers, where they face Mexico on Tue Jun 30 18:00 PT — a fixture loaded with regional rivalry and genuine uncertainty; on paper neither side commands the other, and Mexico's tournament experience makes this a true coin-flip. Survive that, and England or DR Congo await in the last sixteen, with Brazil or Norway a possible quarterfinal assignment that would represent the sternest examination of just how far Ecuador's momentum can carry them.

Egypt.text: Egypt edged through Group G in nervy fashion, taking a fifth-minute lead against Iran through Saber only to be pegged back almost immediately by Rezaeian — a draw that secured qualification but hardly announced an emerging force. The Pharaohs face Australia in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a tie that looks manageable on paper but demands a sharper, more sustained performance than Egypt have yet produced. Survive that, and Argentina could await in the Round of 16 — a formidable step up that would expose precisely how far this side has genuinely come.

England.text: England closed the group stage with a controlled 2-0 win over Panama, Bellingham and Kane striking in quick succession midway through the second half to seal a comfortable, professional finish to Group L. They meet DR Congo in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 09:00 PT — a tie England will be expected to navigate with authority, though African sides at this tournament have shown the capacity to unsettle heavier favourites. Survive that, and a potential quarterfinal collision with Brazil or Norway looms on Sat Jul 11 14:00 PT — the kind of assignment that would measure exactly how serious this England side is.

France.text: Dembélé dismantled Norway almost single-handedly, completing a first-half hat-trick inside 32 minutes to put the tie beyond doubt before Doué added gloss deep in stoppage time — a 4-1 scoreline that barely required France to break sweat. Les Bleus top Group I with a perfect nine points and a commanding goal difference, arriving at the knockout rounds in ominous form. Sweden await in the Round of 32 on Tue Jun 30 14:00 PT, a winnable assignment for a side of France's pedigree, though Paraguay in the last sixteen on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT will demand more concentration. Further down the draw, Canada or Morocco could provide a genuine quarterfinal examination, with Spain or Portugal lurking as potential semifinal adversaries if France navigate that far.

Germany.text: Germany's Round of 32 exit is as brutal as the format can produce: Havertz's second-half equaliser cancelled out Enciso's opener, and when neither side could be separated across 90 minutes, Paraguay held their nerve in the shootout, converting four of four while Germany stumbled to three. A group-stage winner undone before the last sixteen, Joachim Löw's side — or whoever currently stewards this generation — departs a tournament they entered with genuine ambitions, beaten not by a superior team across the pitch but by the cold lottery of penalties.

Ghana.text: Ghana limped into the knockout rounds on the back of a defeat, Croatia's Nikola Vlasic punishing them with a late strike to seal a 2-1 victory after Luckassen had briefly levelled the match in the 73rd minute — a familiar story of promise undone by fragility. They survived as one of the best third-placed sides, which speaks to the tightness of Group L rather than any commanding form. Colombia await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a genuinely difficult draw against a side with considerably more momentum, leaving Ghana as clear underdogs with work to do. Survive that, and Algeria or Switzerland could follow — before the prospect of Argentina looms in the quarterfinal.

Haiti.text: Haiti depart the 2026 World Cup without a point to their name, their group stage exit confirmed by a 4-2 defeat to Morocco that was comfortable for the Atlas Lions despite a spirited Haitian showing either side of half-time. Two goals inside the first 43 minutes gave Haiti brief cause for optimism, but Morocco reasserted themselves emphatically in the closing stages, scoring four times in total to send their opponents home. The Caribbeans finish bottom of Group C, undone by a gulf in quality that proved too wide to bridge across three matches.

Iran.text: Iran's group-stage finale against Egypt ended 1-1, a Rezaeian equaliser on 14 minutes cancelling out Saber's early opener — a pattern that defined their entire tournament: always in the contest, never quite through it. Three draws, no wins, and three points were enough to sneak through as one of the best third-placed sides, but the knockout round delivered the reckoning that the group stage had deferred. Iran exit the 2026 World Cup without a victory to their name, their campaign a study in stubborn resilience that ultimately fell short of the cutting edge a deep run demands.

Iraq.text: Iraq's group stage ended in humiliation against Senegal, a 5-0 defeat that was effectively settled inside the opening minutes and then ruthlessly extended across the second half, with Gueye alone scoring twice in a 12-minute spell. Finishing fourth in Group I with three losses, no points, and twelve goals conceded, Iraq's tournament was over at the earliest stage — a chastening introduction to a World Cup where the gulf in class between the continent's elite and the tournament's newcomers was laid painfully bare.

Ivory Coast.text: Ivory Coast gave Norway a genuine contest in the Round of 32, pulling level through Diallo on 74 minutes after falling behind to Nusa's first-half opener — only for Haaland to settle it with four minutes remaining, a late hammer blow that extinguished the comeback. The Elephants depart a tournament they had navigated with enough promise to suggest they belonged, yet ultimately could not withstand Norway's cutting edge when it mattered most.

Japan.text: Japan held their own against Brazil for the better part of an hour, Sano's 29th-minute opener giving them genuine belief before Casemiro levelled midway through the second half. It was Martinelli's stoppage-time winner that delivered the cruellest of exits — a Samurai Blue side that had navigated Group F without defeat ultimately undone in the final seconds of a Round of 32 tie they had come agonisingly close to taking further.

Jordan.text: Jordan's group stage ended with a 3-1 defeat to Argentina — a result that followed the script almost from the first whistle, with the South Americans establishing a two-goal cushion inside half an hour before Messi put the contest beyond doubt late on. Al-Tamari's consolation just after the hour offered a brief flicker, but Jordan were outclassed. Finishing fourth in Group J with three defeats and no points, they nevertheless advanced as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides before their World Cup came to a close.

Mexico.text: Mexico swept through Group A without conceding a goal, and their 3-0 dismissal of Czech Republic — settled by a brace in quick succession after the break and a late third to ice it — had the comfortable authority of a side moving through the gears rather than pushing at their limits. Ecuador await in the Round of 32 on Tue Jun 30 18:00 PT, a winnable tie that Mexico enter as clear favourites, though the Ecuadorians are a physical, well-organised South American outfit capable of making life uncomfortable. Win that, and England could loom in the Round of 16 — a genuine examination of whether this Mexican side's clean-sheet poise holds against elite European opposition.

Morocco.text: Morocco survived the most dramatic of finishes against the Netherlands — level at 1-1 after a last-gasp Diop equaliser deep in stoppage time cancelled out Gakpo's 72nd-minute opener, before the Atlas Lions held their nerve in a 3-2 shootout. They meet Canada in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT, a tie that sets up as a manageable assignment on paper — Canada are energetic and ambitious hosts but Morocco, with their defensive solidity and late-game resilience, enter as clear favourites. Should they progress, France or Sweden could await in the quarterfinal, with Portugal or Spain lurking as potential semifinal opposition — a genuinely daunting stretch of the bracket for any side.

Netherlands.text: Netherlands dominated their group with seven points and a healthy goal difference, only to suffer one of the tournament's crueller exits — Gakpo's 72nd-minute strike cancelled out by a Moroccan equaliser deep in stoppage time, before the Dutch lost the subsequent shootout 2-3. It was the worst kind of departure: a lead surrendered at the death, then the lottery of penalties delivering the final blow. A side that had looked like genuine contenders through the group stage goes home from the Round of 32, undone in the space of twenty minutes.

New Zealand.text: Belgium administered a thorough lesson in the final group game, with Trossard's brace and further goals from De Bruyne and Lukaku turning what might have been a contest into a rout — New Zealand's consolation arriving far too late, in the 84th minute, to suggest any real foothold. The All Whites finished fourth in Group G with a single point and a goals difference of minus ten, their tournament record a frank reflection of the gulf between them and the competition's established forces. They depart a World Cup that offered little comfort beyond the experience itself.

Norway.text: Norway edged past Ivory Coast in a nervy Round of 32 tie, Nusa's first-half opener holding until Diallo levelled with quarter of an hour to play before Haaland rescued it with a late winner four minutes from time. The reward is a Round of 16 date with Brazil on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT — a formidable assignment by any measure, with the Seleção among the tournament's heavyweights and Norway cast firmly as underdogs, though Haaland's late intervention serves notice that they carry a threat to the last breath. Survive that, and England or Argentina could await deeper in the draw.

Panama.text: Panama's group stage exit was as comprehensive as the record suggests — three defeats, no goals scored, and a closing loss to England that was settled efficiently in a five-minute spell midway through the second half, Bellingham and Kane putting the game to bed without drama or late anxiety. They finish bottom of Group L with nothing to show from their three outings, and whatever hope they carried into the tournament evaporated long before the knockout rounds began. A familiar story for Central American football at this level: the gap between qualifying and competing proved too wide to bridge.

Paraguay.text: Paraguay pulled off one of the tournament's genuine upsets, taking Germany to the wire and holding their nerve in a penalty shootout — Enciso's composed finish before half-time gave them the lead, Havertz levelled early in the second half, but the Albirroja held firm through extra time and edged it 4-3 from the spot. Now they face either France or Sweden in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, with France representing one of the heaviest assignments left in the draw and Sweden a more navigable but far from comfortable proposition. Survive that, and Canada or Morocco could await in the quarterfinals — a bracket that, improbably, keeps the path to a semifinal just about in sight.

Portugal.text: Portugal navigated a cautious finale to the group stage, holding Colombia to a goalless draw that secured second place in Group K without ever threatening to light up the scoresheet. Croatia await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT — a genuine fifty-fifty between two experienced European sides, with Portugal's superior attacking depth perhaps giving them a narrow edge over a Croatian generation that may finally be showing its age. Survive that, and either Spain or Austria loom in the Round of 16, with a potential quarterfinal against Belgium or the United States adding further steel to what could become a brutal second half of the draw.

Qatar.text: Qatar's group stage exit arrived with little ceremony — a 1-3 defeat to Bosnia & Herzegovina in which they trailed by two goals before half-time, Al-Haydos pulling one back on the stroke of the interval before Mahmic's 80th-minute strike killed any lingering hope of a comeback. The hosts finished bottom of Group B with a single point and a goal difference of minus eight, their tournament a sobering exercise in the gap between ambition and execution at this level.

Saudi Arabia.text: Saudi Arabia's group stage ended with a goalless draw against Cape Verde — a stalemate that was as flat as the scoreline suggests, neither side able to manufacture a breakthrough across ninety minutes. That result, combined with a goal difference of minus five across three matches, proved just enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed teams, but the Green Falcons were eliminated in the Round of 32, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Scotland.text: Brazil ended Scotland's tournament before it had truly begun, a 3-0 defeat that was every bit as one-sided as the scoreline suggests. Júnior struck twice in the first half — the second arriving deep in added time — before Cunha added a third shortly after the interval, leaving Scotland with nothing to build on. They depart having advanced from the group stage as one of the best third-placed sides, a modest distinction that flatters a campaign yielding just one win and four goals conceded. The 2026 World Cup ends for Scotland at the Round of 32.

Senegal.text: Senegal closed their group stage with a statement, dismantling Iraq 5-0 in a performance that grew increasingly ruthless as the second half wore on — Gueye's brace and a late Ndiaye finish turning a comfortable lead into a rout. They advanced as one of the best third-placed sides, which sets up a Round of 32 encounter with Belgium on Wed Jul 1 13:00 PT — a genuine test against a side with far greater knockout pedigree, though Senegal's attacking fluency will give the Belgians pause. Survive that, and Portugal or Spain could await in the quarterfinal.

South Africa.text: South Africa's World Cup ended in the cruelest fashion, a 90th-minute goal from Eustáquio giving Canada a 1-0 win that the balance of the match may not have fully demanded. Bafana Bafana had navigated the group stage with enough grit to finish second in Group A, but a tournament played on home soil — or at least in their confederate hemisphere — ultimately closed in the dying seconds of a Round of 32 tie they very nearly survived.

South Korea.text: South Korea close their World Cup 2026 account with a flat 0-1 defeat to South Africa, Maseko's goal just past the hour mark enough to settle a match the Taeguk Warriors never truly threatened to turn. They had squeezed through the group phase as one of the best third-placed sides, but that slender lifeline ran out in the round of 32 — eliminated without a goal scored in their final two outings, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Spain.text: Spain closed the group stage with a controlled 1-0 win over Uruguay, Álex Baena's first-half strike — delivered just before the break — proving sufficient for a side that conceded nothing across three matches and topped Group H with their composure intact. Austria arrive on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT for the Round of 32, and while the Austrians are a tidy, well-organized side, Spain enter as firm favorites; this should be a manageable assignment for a team playing with the quiet authority of genuine contenders. Survive that, and the draw opens onto more serious terrain — Croatia or Portugal could await in the Round of 16, a potential meeting with France looms in the semifinals, and a final against Argentina or Brazil would complete the story in the grandest possible fashion.

Sweden.text: Sweden scraped through Group F's closing round in unconvincing fashion, trailing to Daizen Maeda's 56th-minute strike before Anthony Elanga levelled six minutes later to salvage a 1-1 draw with Japan — enough, by the slimmest of margins, to advance as one of the best third-placed sides. The reward is a Round of 32 assignment against France on Tue Jun 30 14:00 PT, a fixture in which Sweden enter as pronounced underdogs against one of the tournament's genuine title contenders; surviving would rank among the upsets of the competition. Clear that hurdle and Paraguay await in the Round of 16, before a potential quarterfinal against Canada or Morocco offers a more navigable, though hardly trivial, path deeper into the draw.

Switzerland.text: Switzerland finished Group B in style, turning aside Canada 2-1 with a composed second-half display — goals from Vargas and Manzambi either side of the hour giving them control before David's late consolation added a flicker of anxiety to an otherwise assured performance. The Swiss meet Algeria in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a tie that favors Murat Yakin's side on reputation and form, though Algeria are capable of unsettling more fancied opponents on the counter. Survive that, and a potential quarterfinal collision with Argentina looms as the draw's most daunting prospect from Switzerland's half of the bracket.

Tunisia.text: Tunisia's group stage exit was written early: two Dutch goals inside seven minutes buried the Carthage Eagles before the match had found its shape, and though Mastouri pulled one back after the break, Hecke's strike shortly after the hour restored the three-goal cushion and sealed a 3-1 defeat. Tunisia finished bottom of Group F without a point, their record of three defeats and a goal difference of minus ten telling the story of a tournament in which they were consistently outclassed — a dispiriting campaign ended against one of the competition's heavyweights.

Turkey.text: Turkey's tournament ends in the cruelest of fashions — a 3-2 win over the United States that arrived too late to matter. They trailed inside three minutes, fought back to lead through Güler and Yilmaz, conceded again early in the second half, and only sealed it through Ayhan's injury-time strike. The drama was real, the effort undeniable, but a fourth-place group finish — even as one of the better third-placed sides — proved an insufficient foundation, and Turkey's World Cup 2026 is over at the group stage.

USA.text: USA topped Group D despite a bruising final group match against Turkey — Trusty's third-minute opener gave way to a Turkish surge, and an agonising 90+8' winner from Ayhan made for a nervy exit from the phase, though the Americans had already done enough to advance. Bosnia & Herzegovina await in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 17:00 PT, a fixture that sets up as a manageable assignment for the hosts, whose firepower and home advantage should tell against a Bosnian side still finding its footing on the world stage. Survive that, and Belgium or Senegal loom in the Round of 16, with the possibility of a heavyweight quarterfinal collision against Portugal or Spain beyond.

Uruguay.text: Uruguay's group stage ended with a narrow 0-1 defeat to Spain, Álex Baena's first-half strike the solitary difference in a contest that offered the Uruguayans little joy in the final third. Despite advancing as one of the best third-placed sides, their tournament came to an abrupt halt at the same juncture — two draws and a loss across Group H, with a negative goal difference, telling a story of a side that never quite found its footing at this World Cup.

Uzbekistan.text: Uzbekistan's group stage ended with a 1-3 defeat to DR Congo, a result that traced a familiar arc: an encouraging early lead through Shomurodov, then a second-half capitulation as Wissa and Mayele dismantled them after the hour mark, Wissa adding a third in stoppage time to put a harsh gloss on the scoreline. The Central Asians finished bottom of Group K without a point, conceding eleven goals across three matches, and no late reprieve arrived. Their debut on the expanded World Cup stage was brief and bruising, a tournament that exposed the gulf between ambition and readiness at the highest level.
